One thing I've realized while transitioning into cybersecurity:  The best SOC analysts don't memorize malware—they understand attacker behavior.  When I read an incident report, I try to answer five questions:  • How did initial access happen? • What telemetry would detect it first? • Which identities were targeted? • Where could defenders have interrupted the attack? • What detection rule would I improve tomorrow?  Recent disruption of the StealC and Amadey ecosystem is a reminder that cybercrime is highly organized. Malware delivery, credential theft, and ransomware are often handled by different groups working together. The more I study, the more I believe that understanding the attack chain is more valuable than memorizing malware names. That's the mindset I'm building every day.  #CyberSecurity #BlueTeam #SOC #ThreatDetection #DetectionEngineering #DFIR
Attackers deleted their tracks. Or so they thought.  Artifact of the Day | DFIR Media  #DFIR #CyberSecurity #InfoSec

macOS Unified Logs are the single most comprehensive forensic artifact on modern Apple systems — and most responders barely scratch the surface.  Introduced in macOS 10.12 (Sierra), Unified Logs replaced the legacy Apple System Log (ASL) with a high-performance, structured logging subsystem. Every process, kernel extension, and framework writes to this centralized store. You get process execution, network connections, Bluetooth pairing, USB device attachment, application behavior, authentication events, and more — all timestamped to the nanosecond.  Logs live in /var/db/diagnostics/ (active) and /var/db/uuidtext/ (metadata). The native 'log show' command is your first-line tool, but it's slow on large datasets. Use predicates to filter efficiently:  log show --predicate 'process == "ssh"' --info --debug  For forensic analysis, UnifiedLogReader (Python) and mac_apt parse the binary .tracev3 files into human-readable formats. Both support timeline export and keyword filtering — critical when hunting for MITRE T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) activity or lateral movement indicators.  Practical IR use case: Detecting unauthorized remote access. Filter for 'sshd' or 'screensharingd' process names, correlate with authentication subsystem messages, and cross-reference timestamps with network logs. You'll surface SSH sessions, VNC connections, and Apple Remote Desktop activity — even if the attacker cleaned bash history.  Unified Logs are verbose, noisy, and invaluable. Master the predicate syntax and you'll cut triage time in half.  #DFIR #IncidentResponse

That Microsoft login link in your inbox? It might be malware — and there's no typo to catch.  Our team analyzed an active, in-the-wild attack that turns a genuine `http://login[.]microsoftonline[.]com` URL into a malware delivery system. No look-alike domain. No misspelling. Real Microsoft cert. It sails past the "hover the link and check the domain" advice we've all been giving for years.  The trick: attackers abuse Microsoft's own OAuth sign-in flow as an open redirect. The malicious destination never appears in the link — Microsoft supplies it after the click.  Where it ends up is worse: a rogue Ninite Remote agent installed as SYSTEM, then TeamViewer and AnyDesk layered on top for redundant, hands-on remote control.  We break down exactly how the redirect works (in plain language), why your URL filters wave it through, and how to hunt it 👇  📖 Full analysis + IOCs: na2.hubs.ly/H06nFRD0 #ThreatIntel #DFIR #Phishing #OAuth #IncidentResponse #RMM #CyberSecurity
In Arlington, VA this year in the fall. Join us. Fresh voices, concepts, and ideas headed your way. #DFIR

디지털 보안관이라고 해킹 탐지나 포렌식, 서버 관리 ··· 다양하게 하고 있거든요

sample_tweets.text: [사설] 공정·신뢰 무너뜨린 축협·선관위 카르텔, 감사원의 고강도 감사로 단죄하라  공정성과 절차적 정당성이 생명인 두 핵심 국가 시스템의 대들보가 동시에 무너졌다. 대한축구협회(축협)의 홍명보 전 감독 특혜 선임과 중앙선거관리위원회(선관위)의 6·3 지방선거 부실 관리 사태가 그것이다.  두 사건의 본질은 하나다. 폐쇄적 인맥 카르텔과 오만한 선민의식이 결탁해 공적 기능을 사유화하고, 외부 감시가 없는 사각지대에서 '돈과 이권'을 챙겼다는 점이다. 내부 구조가 폐쇄적일수록 외부 이해관계자와의 검은 유착은 깊어질 수밖에 없다.   이번 사태를 단순 행정 과실이 아닌 구조적 국기문란으로 규정하고 철저히 파헤쳐야 하는 이유다.  ✅ 축협의 '밀실 선임', 행정 미숙 아닌 명백한 채용비리다  홍명보 전 감독 선임은 축협의 주장처럼 단순 행정 미숙이 아닌 명백한 '채용비리'관점에서 다루어져야 마땅하다. 전력강화위원회의 정상적 서류 면접과 평가 순위를 수뇌부가 임의로 무력화하고, 심야 밀실에서 특정인을 낙점한 행위는 공정한 경쟁 기회를 박탈한 부정 채용의 전형이다.  축협 수뇌부가 비난을 자초하며 선임을 밀어붙인 배경에는 막대한 상업적 자금력이 도사리고 있다. 국가대표 감독직은 수십억 연봉을 넘어 수백억 원 규모의 중계권, 스폰서십 등 이권이 결부된 비즈니스의 정점이기 때문이다. 특정 학연 카르텔이 대중을 기만한 배후에는 마케팅 이권을 나눠 먹으려는 외부 에이전트나 기업형 이해관계자와의 유착 의혹이 짙다. 문체부 감사와 법원 판결로 위법성이 드러난 만큼, 금전 거래 유무를 밝힐 고강도 감사가 시급하다.  ✅ '투표지 부족' 선관위 사태, 정치세력의 '선거 조작 사주' 가능성 규명하라  선관위의 6·3 지방선거 '투표용지 50% 축소 지침'과 투표지 부족 사태는 단순 과실을 넘어선 '고의적 선거 조작'의 관점으로 보아야 한다.   유권자의 참정권을 물리적으로 제한한 엄중한 국기문란 범죄이기 때문이다. 그동안 자녀 특혜 채용 세습 등으로 조직을 사유화해 온 선관위의 도덕적 붕괴는 선거 관리 시스템의 치명적 맹점으로 이어졌다.  매 선거마다 수천억 원의 선거 예산을 집행하며 전자개표기 도입, 보안·인쇄 계약 등 발주 권한을 독점하는 과정에서 특정 정치세력과 유착된 외부 보안·IT 기업들이 이권을 나눠 가졌을 가능성이 있다.  특히 이처럼 얽히고설킨 검은 유착 관계가 단순한 금전적 이익 공유를 넘어, 특정 정치세력으로부터 선거 결과를 인위적으로 조작하라는 구체적인 사주를 받고 실행에 옮겼을 가능성 또한 결코 배제할 수 없다.  돈과 권력에 눈먼 폐쇄적 카르텔이 특정 정치세력의 승리를 보장해 주는 대가로 고의적인 선거 조작의 빌미를 제공했다는 의혹은 이번 감사에서 치밀하게 파헤쳐야 할 핵심 과제다.  ✅ 사법부의 '판사 카르텔'과 금전 만능주의가 낳은 연쇄적 붕괴  노태악 전 위원장 등 사법부 출신 엘리트들의 행태는 사법 정의의 붕괴와 직결된다. 대한민국 판사들은 법관 시절의 명예와 특권을 방패 삼아 '판사 카르텔'을 형성하고, 퇴직 후에는 '전관예우'라는 통로를 통해 수십억 원의 수임료를 쓸어 담는다.  이러한 추악한 금전욕이 판결문의 신뢰를 무너뜨렸다. 재판의 공정성이 아닌 '전관의 영향력'에 따라 판결이 뒤바뀌는 현실 속에서, 선관위의 도덕적 해이 역시 사법부의 고질적인 금전 만능주의와 특권의식이 행정 및 선거 영역으로 전이된 필연적 결과물일 뿐이다.  ✅ 감사원, '독립기관' 방패 허물고 무제한적 특별직무감찰 단행해야  무너진 시스템을 바로잡을 유일한 해결책은 감사원의 강력한 메스뿐이다. 헌법상 독립기관을 핑계로 감사를 거부해 온 선관위의 기만과, 시간 끌기로 일관하는 축협의 오만은 이제 끝내야 한다. 감사원은 두 사태를 연계해 내부 채용비리, 선거관리 위법 행위, 검은 돈의 흐름과 배후 세력을 밝힐 특별직무감찰을 즉각 단행해야 한다.  의혹이 불거진 외부 IT·보안업체 및 대형 에이전트와의 계약 전 과정을 현미경 검증하듯 전수조사해야 마땅하다. 아울러 디지털 포렌식 기법을 동원해 내부 문서와 유착 정황이 담긴 통신·로그 기록까지 낱낱이 확보하는 전례 없는 고강도 감사가 필요하다.   카르텔의 지독한 금전욕과 유착 의혹을 감사원의 타협 없는 메스로 철저히 허물지 못한다면, 대한민국의 민주주의와 국가 시스템의 신뢰는 영원히 회복될 수 없을 것이다.

38-Year-Old Zimbabwean National Arrested After Police Recover 151 Protected Cycads Worth R1.5 Million During Late-Night Operation Near Kariega as Two Suspects Escape  A 38-year-old Zimbabwean national has been arrested after police allegedly found him in possession of 151 protected cycads valued at approximately R1.5 million near Kariega. The arrest followed a police operation launched after a local farmer reported suspicious activity involving the removal of cycads from a farm.  According to police, the incident began on Monday, 29 June 2026, when a farmer alerted authorities after seeing several suspects removing cycads from a farm and transporting them towards the R75 Road, about 15 to 20 kilometres outside Kariega. The information prompted an immediate coordinated response.  Members of the Stock Theft and Endangered Species Unit, assisted by K9 and visible policing units, intercepted a suspicious Mazda BT-50 bakkie at about 23:30. Two suspects fled into nearby bushes, while the driver was arrested. Police also recovered the protected cycads and a Samsung cellphone during the operation.  The arrested suspect is expected to appear in the Kariega Magistrate’s Court on charges relating to the alleged contravention of the National Environmental Management: Biodiversity Act. Police said investigations are continuing as efforts remain underway to locate and arrest the two suspects who escaped.

Four men have admitted their part in the CS spray attack at Heathrow Airport last December.  On 7 December, 21 people were sprayed with a noxious substance in the multi-storey car park at Terminal 3, in an incident that sparked an armed police response. Five people needed further hospital treatment. The court heard the spray was used as two suitcases were stolen from the victims.  The four men have all pleaded guilty to affray at Isleworth Crown Court. Affray is a public order offence. In simple terms, it's using or threatening violence in public in a way that would make people nearby genuinely fear for their safety.  CS spray is a chemical used by police to incapacitate people, similar to pepper spray. It can cause burning to the eyes, coughing and chest tightness, and is sometimes described as tear gas.  Police said the incident was not treated as terrorism. The four men will be sentenced on 11 September. #ub1ub2 #london #heathrow  📍 100+ local discounts on the UB1UB2 Card: link in our bio

On September 26, 1983, the Soviet early warning system reported the launch of several U.S. intercontinental ballistic missiles. According to protocol, the information was to be immediately passed to the Soviet leadership, potentially triggering a retaliatory response.  The duty officer, Lieutenant Colonel Stanislav P., questioned the warning's accuracy. Believing it to be a false alarm, he chose not to report it as a confirmed nuclear attack. Shortly afterward, it was discovered that the satellites had mistaken sunlight reflecting off high-altitude clouds for missile launches.  The incident remained classified for years and only became public in the 1990s. Today, it is regarded as one of the most dangerous moments of the Cold War, when the world came within moments of a possible nuclear catastrophe because of a technical error.  #PuzzlesOfHistory

sample_tweets.text: Former Israeli military chief accuses Netanyahu of lying about Iran nuclear program to maintain power —— Former Israeli army chief of staff Gadi Eisenkot has accused Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of distorting facts regarding Iran's nuclear program to induce public panic and ensure his political survival. Speaking at the Herzliya Conference, Eisenkot, who recently launched the Yashar party, directly disputed Netanyahu’s assertions about neutralizing an active Iranian nuclear threat.  "Netanyahu stated things that are untrue; Iran had no nuclear bomb," Eisenkot declared, characterizing the prime minister's security narrative as a political tool aimed at manipulating public fear.  The remarks marked the official launch of Eisenkot’s electoral campaign, during which he stated that the administration responsible for the security failures of 7 October, has reached its end. The former military commander pledged to establish a formal commission of inquiry into the intelligence lapses surrounding the Al-Aqsa Flood operation if elected.  Responding to political attacks from the ruling Likud party, Eisenkot challenged Netanyahu to a direct public debate, demanding that the prime minister answer questions regarding his wartime leadership and domestic policies.  Eisenkot's entry into the campaign has altered internal political projections, with recent opinion polls showing his Yashar party capturing 19 Knesset seats. For the first time, polling indicates Eisenkot leading Netanyahu on suitability for the premiership, securing 38 percent of support compared to Netanyahu’s 35 percent.

Indiana Said Yes — What the New Homestead Vendor Law Means Starting Today  A food business license in Indiana's most regulated counties ran homesteaders between $150 and $400 annually — before the first jar of jam left the property. The annual health department inspection was extra.  As of this morning, that math has changed.  ---  Indiana's House Enrolled Act 1424 took effect July 1. Any homestead vendor or small farm operation under $1.5 million in annual gross sales — roughly 90 percent of Indiana farms — is now exempt from state and local health department permits, licensing, certifications, and routine inspections when selling directly to consumers. Prepared foods, baked goods, jams, produce, eggs, and certain meats from on-farm-raised animals: cattle, swine, sheep, goats, rabbits, bison. All of it, from property or farmers market, without advance permission from a government agency.  Apparently the default assumption had been that a home kitchen is a threat that requires pre-approval before operating.  Labeling requirements remain — ingredients, allergens, that it was made in an unlicensed kitchen. Complaints can still trigger investigation. What's gone is the inspection as the price of entry. The licensing fee as a barrier before the first customer.  ---  The pattern worth watching isn't just what Indiana did. It's how.  Iowa introduced a similar bill this session — HF 2767, which would have expanded raw milk sales on-farm and allowed cottage food products in grocery stores. It passed the House. It did not receive a Senate vote before the session ended. Georgia's Farmstead Relief Act — small dairy producers selling butter and cheese direct from their farms — passed the Georgia House. It died in the Georgia Senate Agriculture committee without a floor vote.  Indiana's bill cleared both chambers. Iowa's and Georgia's didn't.  Some people notice that pattern and conclude the issue is unpopular. It isn't. State House seats are smaller, more directly responsive to the individual constituent who shows up to a committee hearing. State Senate Agriculture committees are where a smaller number of people vote on a larger range of policy, and where industry relationships have had more time to develop. The Institute for Justice — which advocated for HEA 1424 — has documented similar dynamics in food freedom legislation across more than a dozen states. The resistance isn't at the ballot. It's in the committee room after the door closes.  ---  For the homesteading community, the Indiana law is a template as much as it is a policy change.  The language in HEA 1424 — the threshold model, the exemption structure, the limited regulatory carve-outs that made it politically survivable — is the kind of bill text other state coalitions will adapt. West Virginia expanded cottage food last month. Oklahoma expanded raw milk sales earlier this year. Each one creates a precedent, a legal argument, and a working example that the next state's advocates can point to when the committee chair says it can't be done.  Via @IJ_org — the Institute for Justice has published the full legal framework behind Indiana's food freedom approach and what it means for constitutional challenges in other states.  The Farm-to-Consumer Legal Defense Fund (@farmtoconsumer) tracks every state's current law and the 2026 session outcomes in one place. That map is worth knowing before the next session starts.  ---  It's interesting how long the assumption persisted that selling food you grew and prepared on your own land to a neighbor who wanted to buy it was the activity that required government oversight at the front end.  Indiana decided that assumption was worth revisiting. The question other states are working out is whether their Senate Agriculture committees agree.  ---  There's a version of this story that most people never see. It lives in the data, the policy filings, the quiet shifts nobody's naming yet. The Farm2Table Insiders channel is where we pull back the curtain — the raw signals, the sources, and the intelligence behind what you read here.  👉 t.me/farm2tableinsiders #FoodFreedom #Homesteading #BackyardFarming #CottageFoodLaw #SelfReliance #FarmToTable  📖 substack.com/profile/1524765…

14/14 - The Verdict: Expectations Versus Reality AI is delivering transformative tools that boost productivity and unlock new creative possibilities. But it isn't delivering the existential breakthroughs or job apocalypse that headlines promised. The fervour stems from our projections—ancient desires for oracles, gods, and answers to life's biggest questions—not from what the technology actually does. The challenge now is recalibrating expectations. AI is a powerful instrument, not a panacea or a threat to human existence. This brings us to the core problem. AI does not create knowledge. It is an extremely powerful tool to parse, summarize and present us with existing information. Creating knowledge requires human intelligence. Only if information is brought together transparently, is immutable and is verified by a human does it become knowledge! Just as only a human can claim copyright of a work, only knowledge verified and certified by a human can become knowledge. By understanding its real capabilities and limits, we can harness its benefits without succumbing to hype-driven disillusionment or the erosion of trust that deepfakes and misinformation bring. The AI boom will endure, but only if we see it clearly.

【情報操作・国家主導IO】ウクライナ侵攻で鍛えられたロシアの影響力エコシステムが再びグローバル展開へ——GTIGが構造分析を公開  Google Threat Intelligence Group（GTIG）は、ロシアの影響力工作（IO）エコシステムの包括的な構造分析を公開した。ウクライナ侵攻開始から4年、戦争を契機に急拡大・高度化したプロロシアのIOが、再び欧米・NATO・EU・旧ソ連圏への広域展開へと軸足を移しつつあるという評価が核心だ。  エコシステムは6つのコアコンポーネントから成る。①ロシア政府（政策・ナラティブの源泉）、②国営メディア（RTなど公式プロパガンダ）、③秘密IO（DoppelgangerやSecondary Infektionなど大規模偽情報キャンペーン）、④ハクティビスト（NoName057(16)など、一部GRUと連携）、⑤サイバースパイ＋IO融合操作（UNC4057によるハック＆リーク等）、⑥サードパーティ請負業者（NTC VulkanなどIOインフラ開発の外注先）である。これらは独立して動きながらも相互に強化し合い、露見後も活動を継続する高い持続性を備える。  戦術面では、ジェネレーティブAIをIO全ライフサイクル（企画・リサーチ・コンテンツ生成）に活用する傾向が加速している。社会内の既存の分断に乗じる「ナラティブ共鳴」戦術、メール・SMSを用いた直接拡散、正規メディアに偽装したメディアミミクリも引き続き駆使される。「Operation Overload」などの後継キャンペーンはウクライナ支援を超えた地政学的目標にすでに転用済みであり、今後の大規模選挙・オリンピック・中東情勢等への乗じた活動強化が警戒される。 cloud.google.com/blog/topics…

Hacker enthüllen: Ukraine verlor seit 2022 über 2,4 Millionen Soldaten  Nach Erkenntnissen von Hackergruppen soll die Ukraine seit Kriegsbeginn 2022 über 2,4 Millionen Soldaten verloren haben. Die aus angezapften Datenbanken des Generalstabs, der Rekrutierungszentren TCC, von Krankenhäusern und Leichenhallen stammenden Zahlen zeigen eine verheerende Entwicklung, die von der Kiewer Regierung stets abgestritten wird. Bereits bis August 2025 wurden demnach 1,7 Millionen getötete oder vermißte Soldaten gezählt, im Dezember 2025 überschritt die Verluststatistik die Zwei-Millionen-Marke. Allein im ersten Halbjahr 2026 seien erneut rund 400.000 Mann gefallen – so viele wie im gesamten Jahr 2023. Die durchschnittliche Verlustquote wird in den Dateien auf etwa 500 Soldaten pro Tag beziffert.   Die Datenlecks werden den Hackergruppen PalachPro und NoName057(16) zugeschrieben. PalachPro soll bereits tausende Überwachungskameras in der Ukraine lahmgelegt haben, NoName057(16) zeichnet für großangelegte DDoS-Attacken auf ukrainische Einrichtungen verantwortlich. Die Zahlen, sollten sie zutreffend sein, übertreffen alle bisher öffentlich bekannten Schätzungen bei weitem.   Also solche unglaublichen Verluste an der Ukraine. Und unsere Regierung tut nur, dass sie den tatsächlichen Tod von Menschen unterstützt. Außerdem berauben sie uns, um all das zu finanzieren. So oder so, aber dieser Wahnsinn muss aufhören! So wird es definitiv besser sein, sowohl für uns als auch für die Ukraine.

The NVIDIA Inception program is a free, equity-free accelerator designed to help AI, data science, and high-performance computing startups accelerate growth. It provides members with technical resources, hardware discounts, cloud credits, and investor networking.Key BenefitsHardware & Software Perks: Preferred pricing on select NVIDIA hardware and software, plus early access to the latest developer tools and SDKs. Cloud Credits: Access to cloud and partner credits (such as AWS Activate) ranging from $5,000 to $100,000 depending on your funding and startup profile.Technical Training: Free credits for courses and certifications through the NVIDIA Deep Learning Institute (DLI).Investor & Go-To-Market Support: Co-marketing opportunities and access to NVIDIA’s network of venture capitalists (VCs).No Strings Attached: The program does not take any equity or charge participation fees.EligibilityTo qualify for the program, your company must meet the following baseline criteria:Be officially incorporated and less than 10 years old.Employ at least one active developer.Maintain a working business website (generic email addresses like Gmail are not accepted).How to ApplyThe application is virtual and can take anywhere from a few days to a few weeks for approval. You can submit your company's details directly on the NVIDIA Inception Application page.(Note: If you have questions about the application or want to see what past applicants are saying, Reddit users on the Y Combinator Forum generally agree that the application process is quick and equity-free, though opinions can vary on the specific credit amounts rewarded based on your startup stage.)

In a different context, an Indian customer of some of our products told me recently "A big Microsoft Office license renewal came up and they hiked the price drastically. We told them we are looking at the Zoho office suite and they dropped the price by 90%" - he thanked me for saving them big money, even without buying our office suite. If you are facing renewal of Microsoft Office license, I suggest you mention Zoho.  Some history: Microsoft is a convicted monoplist by a US Federal Court (April 2000) and the monopoly finding but not the suggested remedy was upheld by the U.S Court of Appeals (DC circuit) in 2001. Microsoft has a long illustrious history of milking the customer dry (see the AI link below for details).  Competition is always important. The fact that American AI models have serious competition from China is important for India and other countries.   Finally, I am now confident that India will catch up in AI models. There is no reason to be despondent. There are multiple efforts going on in academia and industry and the cost to train AI models is starting to fall. Many countries in Europe have long used unisex or single-stall restrooms without widespread safety issues. The presence of a shared restroom doesn't eliminate privacy, individual stalls are still enclosed, and laws against harassment or assault still apply regardless of who is in the restroom.  Protecting women, men, and children from harassment is important, but there's little evidence that allowing transgender people to use restrooms matching their gender identity increases assaults. Safety should be addressed by enforcing laws against inappropriate behavior, not by assuming an entire group of people is a threat.

🦹‍♂️ Hackers no longer break in, they log in.  You just spent half a million dollars on zero-trust architecture. A teenager with a spoofed phone number and a fake cough just bypassed it in four minutes.  July marks the start of Ransomware Awareness Month, which means we need to stop pretending this is strictly a malware problem. It is fundamentally an identity problem. Bad actors know the softest spot in your armor is usually the helpdesk, relying on human empathy and manufactured urgency to completely bypass your technical controls. All the network encryption in the world means absolutely nothing if someone can simply call IT, claim they lost their phone, and talk their way into a password reset.  Identity verification shuts down that vulnerability. When you demand an automated biometric check for high-risk account recoveries, social engineering hits a wall because you simply cannot sweet-talk a liveness check.  Where is the weakest link in your digital front door right now?

Surf is built by @SurfAI , Inc., a company connected to the earlier Cyber/CyberConnect ecosystem  The team is led by CEO Ryan Li, who has a background from UC Berkeley and experience in both AI and crypto. Other key people behind the project include Shiyu Zhang, Wilson Wei, and Zhimao Liu  Before Surf, the team worked on CyberConnect, a decentralized social graph protocol. Later, the project evolved and Surf AI, Inc. became its own company with a clearer focus on crypto AI  The company is based in Palo Alto, California, and now works around two main directions:  Cyber Network, an Ethereum Layer 2 focused on AI and social infrastructure  Surf, the crypto AI product built to make crypto research easier  Surf also has an internal research and development team called Cybertino Lab, which works on its AI models and crypto intelligence systems  So in simple words:  Surf is not just a random AI chatbot  It comes from a team that has already been building in Web3, social graphs, and crypto infrastructure for years

The actor lists a $500,000 ransom and a deadline of July 15, 2026, threatening to edit the post and sell the data if payment is not made.  Yocale is a Canada-based cloud business management and appointment platform used by service-based businesses to manage bookings, clients, payments, and communications.  Advertised data includes:  • Appointment and booking records • Client names • Client emails and phone numbers • Location and business details • Provider / staff information • Appointment dates and status fields • Payment and invoice-related fields • Timezone and currency fields • Client images and profile references • Business tax and address fields  Claim is unverified.  💥 Stop guessing what's redacted. sample_tweets.text: 🌐 Data theft has become one of the most profitable business models in cybercrime.  ShinyHunters didn't become one of the most recognized names by deploying sophisticated malware. Instead, the group repeatedly demonstrated how exposed credentials, cloud misconfigurations, and weak identity security can lead to breaches affecting hundreds of millions of people.  Our latest research explores how ShinyHunters evolved from selling stolen databases to operating large-scale extortion campaigns, the tactics behind some of its most significant operations, and what organizations can learn from them.  Understanding how data is stolen is just as important as understanding how ransomware spreads.  🔗 Download the Report: threatmon.io/the-business-of… #ThreatIntelligence #CyberThreatIntelligence #DataBreach #CyberSecurity #ShinyHunters #ThreatResearch #ThreatMon

⚠️ ClickFix is changing the way attackers gain initial access by convincing users to execute malicious commands themselves no exploit required.  In our latest blog, we break down how ClickFix campaigns work, why they're proving so effective, and the practical steps organizations can take to reduce the risk.  Read the full analysis: threatmon.io/clickfix-how-a-… #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #ClickFix #SocialEngineering #CyberThreats

🚨 Alleged GameStop Database Leak Exposes 56M Customer Records on Darkweb  A post on an underground forum claims to offer a database containing over 56 million records belonging to GameStop, the US-based video game and entertainment retailer. The actor alleges that the dataset includes comprehensive customer profiles, loyalty program details, and purchase histories.  According to the listing, the exposed data fields include:  *   Personally Identifiable Information (PII): First name, last name, gender, date of birth, email address, phone number, and full physical address (street, city, state, zip code). *   Account Details: Customer ID, account creation date, account status (active/inactive/closed), and preferred gaming platform (PC, PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo Switch). *   Loyalty & Financial Data: Loyalty tier (Pro/Basic), Pro membership dates, points balance, lifetime points earned, trade credit balance, total orders, and lifetime spend in USD. *   Marketing Preferences: Email and SMS opt-in status.  GameStop is a major player in the retail sector, operating thousands of stores across North America and internationally. The exposure of such granular customer data, including financial spending habits and precise home addresses, could facilitate targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and physical stalking if the leak is authentic.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by GameStop or third-party security researchers.

sample_tweets.text: Alleged Unauthenticated RCE for Veeam Backup & Replication Advertised on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is an **unauthenticated SYSTEM-level remote code execution (RCE)** affecting **Veeam Backup & Replication 12.x**.  * According to the post, the alleged exploit:   * Claims to bypass the June 2026 patch for **CVE-2026-44963**   * Requires **no authentication, credentials, or user interaction**   * Allegedly grants **NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM** privileges   * Is said to affect all **12.x** builds, including **12.3.2.4854**   * Claims **Veeam Backup & Replication 13.x** is not affected due to architectural changes  * The seller claims to provide:   * A working proof-of-concept (PoC)   * Exploit source code   * Technical documentation and root cause analysis   * A scanner to identify vulnerable targets  * At the time of writing, these claims remain **unverified**, and there is no public evidence confirming the existence or effectiveness of the alleged exploit.  Analyst Note: Organizations running Veeam Backup & Replication should closely monitor for vendor advisories, restrict management interface exposure to trusted networks, review logs for suspicious activity, and be prepared to apply emergency mitigations if the claims are validated.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Veeam

🔞 Elite Dating Leads Dataset Allegedly Advertised on Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a **large-scale U.S. consumer dating leads dataset** associated with **Elitemate.com**.  According to the forum post:   * Alleged source: **Elitemate.com / elite dating leads**   * Region: **United States**   * Claimed volume: **~1 million records**   * Data type: **consumer lead generation / dating leads**  * The actor claims the exposed data includes:   * Email addresses   * First and last names   * Street addresses   * City, state, and ZIP code   * Phone numbers   * Source attribution fields   * IP addresses  * If authentic, this dataset could support targeted phishing, romance scams, identity profiling, lead resale, spam campaigns, and other social engineering activity.  * **Daily Dark Web has not independently verified** the authenticity of the dataset or confirmed that it originated from Elitemate.com.  Analyst Note: Dating and lead-generation datasets carry elevated privacy risk because contact data may be tied to sensitive personal interests or intent. Even without passwords or payment data, these records can be weaponized for scams and targeted manipulation.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #DataLeak

Alleged StockX Multi-Part Database Advertised on Underground Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a **22.64 million-record** database associated with **StockX**, the U.S.-based sneaker and streetwear marketplace.  * According to the forum post, the alleged leak is divided into **three separate database components**:   * User account and profile database (~6.85 million records)   * Shipping and billing address database (~6.05 million records)   * Platform/application database tables (~9.74 million records)  * The actor claims the exposed data includes:   * User IDs   * Account keys   * Email addresses   * Usernames   * Shipping and billing addresses   * Platform application data   * Additional account-related information  * **Daily Dark Web has not independently verified** the authenticity of the dataset or confirmed that it originated from StockX's systems.  * If validated, the exposure could facilitate account takeover attempts, phishing campaigns, identity theft, fraudulent purchases, and other targeted attacks against affected users.  Analyst Note: Large e-commerce datasets become significantly more valuable when account information is combined with address records, enabling highly convincing social engineering and fraud campaigns.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #StockX

👾 Alleged GameStop Customer Database Advertised for Sale  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a **GameStop customer database** containing more than **56 million records**.  * According to the forum post, the dataset allegedly belongs to **GameStop**, the U.S.-based video game and consumer electronics retailer.  * At the time of posting, the actor had not publicly disclosed the complete data schema or provided detailed information regarding the contents of the database.  * **Daily Dark Web has not independently verified** the authenticity of the dataset or confirmed that it originated from GameStop's systems.  * If the claims are validated, a database of this scale could potentially expose millions of customers to phishing campaigns, credential stuffing, identity theft, financial fraud, and other targeted attacks depending on the types of information contained within the dataset.  Analyst Note: Threat actors frequently advertise large consumer databases before releasing technical details or sample data. Organizations should treat such claims seriously while awaiting independent verification and monitor for evidence of credential abuse or customer-targeted phishing.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #GameStop

🇯🇴 Alleged Source Code of Al-Zaytoonah University of Jordan Shared on Underground Forum  A threat actor has published what they claim is the **source code** of **Al-Zaytoonah University of Jordan**, offering it for free on an underground forum.  According to the post, the leaked archive allegedly contains application source code, including:   * PHP application files   * Administrative modules   * JavaScript source and minified files   * Plugin management components   * Authentication-related files   * Theme and UI modules   * Block management functionality   * Other web application resources  * The post includes a directory listing and a download link, suggesting the archive contains a significant portion of the university's web application.  * **Daily Dark Web has not independently verified** the authenticity of the leaked source code or confirmed that it originated from Al-Zaytoonah University of Jordan.  * If authentic, exposure of application source code could significantly increase the risk of vulnerability discovery, exploitation of hardcoded secrets, authentication bypasses, and future attacks against the university's infrastructure.  Analyst Note: Source code leaks often provide attackers with valuable insight into application architecture, business logic, and security controls, enabling more sophisticated and targeted exploitation even when no sensitive data is directly exposed.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Jordan

🪙 Alleged ExWallets Customer Database Advertised on Underground Forum  A threat actor is advertising an alleged database belonging to **ExWallets**, a cryptocurrency exchange service, claiming the breach contains approximately **1.3 million records**.  * According to the forum post, the actor claims the data originated from **ExWallets.com**, an exchange platform supporting cryptocurrency, e-currency, and fiat currency transactions.  * While the post does not disclose the full schema, databases from cryptocurrency exchange platforms commonly contain customer account information, contact details, transaction-related metadata, and KYC-related records depending on the service and jurisdiction.  * **Daily Dark Web has not independently verified** the authenticity of the dataset or confirmed that it originated from ExWallets.  * If validated, such a breach could expose users to phishing campaigns, cryptocurrency theft attempts, account takeover attacks, identity fraud, and targeted social engineering.  Analyst Note: Cryptocurrency exchanges remain attractive targets because even partial customer datasets can be leveraged to identify crypto holders and conduct highly targeted attacks aimed at stealing digital assets or bypassing account security.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Crypto

Alleged Outro.com Contact Database Shared on Underground Forum  A threat actor has posted what they claim is the **Outro.com contacts database**, advertising approximately **2.6 million records** dating back to **2016–2017**.  According to the post, the dataset includes:   * First and last names   * Email addresses and email domains   * Company names and company domains   * Job titles   * LinkedIn profile URLs   * Cities, states, and countries   * Gender and age information   * Contact enrichment metadata   * Phone numbers   * Secondary email addresses   * Profile photo URLs   * Record creation and update timestamps  * Based on the advertised fields, the dataset appears to consist primarily of enriched business contact and professional profile information rather than consumer account credentials.  * **Daily Dark Web has not independently verified** the authenticity of the dataset or confirmed that it originated from Outro.com.  * If authentic, the information could be used for highly targeted phishing, business email compromise (BEC), executive impersonation, and large-scale reconnaissance campaigns against organizations worldwide.  Analyst Note: Historical contact databases remain valuable to threat actors because professional identities, corporate relationships, and email patterns often persist for years, making them effective resources for social engineering operations.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #DataLeak

🇦🇺 Alleged Paul Carroll Customer Database Advertised for Sale  A threat actor is advertising an alleged **2025 database** belonging to **Paul Carroll**, an Australian footwear retailer, claiming the dataset contains approximately **300,000 customer records**.  * According to the forum post, the advertised data includes:   * Customer IDs and account numbers   * Full names   * Company names and ACNs   * Email addresses   * Mobile and phone numbers   * Dates of birth   * Billing and delivery addresses   * Customer type and account status   * Loyalty program information and points balances   * Credit limits and account balances   * Survey responses   * Account creation and last update timestamps  * Based on the advertised fields, the dataset appears to contain a combination of customer profile, contact, loyalty, and account management information.  * **Daily Dark Web has not independently verified** the authenticity of the dataset or confirmed that it originated from Paul Carroll's systems.  * If the claims are validated, the exposed information could be leveraged for identity theft, targeted phishing campaigns, account takeover attempts, loyalty fraud, and other financial scams.  Analyst Note: Retail databases containing detailed customer profiles, loyalty program data, and extensive contact information remain highly valuable to threat actors due to their usefulness in fraud, credential attacks, and social engineering.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Australia

🇱🇾 Alleged Data Breach Targets Libya's Ministry of Technical & Vocational Education  A threat actor claims to have compromised the **Libya Ministry of Technical & Vocational Education**, advertising a database allegedly containing records for approximately **900,000 students** enrolled across technical institutes and colleges.  According to the forum post, the alleged dataset includes:   * National identification numbers   * Full names (Arabic and English)   * Student phone numbers   * Educational institution names   * Student registration numbers   * Departments and academic specializations   * Academic year and semester information   * Gender and nationality   * Passport information   * Graduation project card details  * The actor claims the database covers multiple educational institutions across Libya and contains both personal identity information and academic records.  * At this time, **Daily Dark Web has not independently verified** the authenticity of the alleged breach or confirmed that the data originated from the Libyan Ministry of Technical & Vocational Education.  * If validated, the exposure could place hundreds of thousands of students at risk of identity theft, targeted phishing, fraud, and long-term misuse of personally identifiable information.  Analyst Note: Educational institutions often store extensive identity records that remain valuable for years. Large-scale exposure of student databases can have lasting consequences due to the longevity of government-issued identifiers and academic records.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Libya

🍔 Alleged Burger King Employee Data Advertised on Underground Forum  A threat actor is advertising an alleged dataset containing information on **506 Burger King employees** in the **United States and United Kingdom**.  According to the post, the data was allegedly extracted through **Restaurant Brands International (RBI)** systems and includes:   * Employee names   * Usernames and email addresses   * User IDs   * Manager relationships   * Account creation and expiration dates   * Last access dates   * Email validation status   * User roles and permission levels   * Restaurant numbers   * Branch IDs, names, and branch codes  * Based on the advertised fields, the dataset appears to consist primarily of employee directory and account management information rather than customer data.  * **Daily Dark Web has not independently verified** the authenticity of the dataset or the claim that it originated from Restaurant Brands International or Burger King systems.  * If confirmed, the exposed information could facilitate targeted phishing, credential harvesting, business email compromise (BEC), and social engineering attacks against employees and franchise operations.  Analyst Note: Even relatively small employee datasets can be valuable to threat actors when they reveal organizational hierarchy, user roles, and internal account metadata that can be leveraged in follow-on attacks.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #BurgerKing

tl;dr really effective malware multi-staged, multiple programming languages, use as many dependencie

sample_tweets.text: Goodnight tiny people living inside my phone

tl;dr really effective malware multi-staged, multiple programming languages, use as many dependencies as possible. AI making this easier to do. AVs struggling  Historically, in regards to malware development, the end goal was minimalism. It was in your best interest to strip as many dependencies, shred the file size down, and make it position independent.  I think, as of ... now ... we need to take a different approach.  I think instead of stripping binaries, we (Red Team, Threat Emulation, malware developers) should intentionally introduce dependencies.  I have witnesses two unique things in the malware landscape since the AI boom.  1. Increase in malware slop. I continue to see stagers which contain notes in them. This is not intentional and this does not "trick" the analyst. This is a colossal mistake on the malware developers part. However, despite it being slop, AI has made malware more diverse. I am seeing more and more malware in Lua, Node JS (including SEA and nexe), Java, and Python. I am seeing more and more malware doing inter-process communication across multiple programming languages. Of course all of these have existed prior to AI, but I am seeing an explosion in these languages. This also has resulted in malware researchers creating new tools to combat this malware diversity.  2. Anti-malware services struggling. When I encounter a binary that is a Node JS SEA blob (Electron JS .exe, self-contained using SEA), which extracts a .JS payload, which uses obfuscated Java or heavily obfuscated Lua, all of these languages require a VM (PVM, LVM, JVM, whatever) for interpretation. Thus, with heavy obfuscation and multistaging, static analysis fails and the heavy abstraction makes it difficult for traditional hooking or minifilters to be effective, in essence there is too much noise. Many of these payloads with heavy dependencies easily avoid static analysis and even some emulation systems because they fail to account for the necessary dependencies which are required to emulate it correctly.  pic maybe related idk
It's 2026 bro, fuck it.  It's time to go to court and sue people over TUNG TUNG SAHOR and U DIN DIN DIN DIN DUN MA DIN DIN DIN DUN, intellectual property of AI slop brainrot, and Roblox mini-games.
omg i got FREE malware from REDDIT
Chat, I don't want to jump to conclusions, but I have a sneaking suspicion this malware stager was vibe coded. Historically, malware hasn't left extremely descriptive comments in their stagers.

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-01 20:28 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

제공해주신 위협 인텔리전스 데이터를 분석한 결과입니다.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 LLM의 환각(Hallucination) 현상을 악용하여 존재하지 않는 도메인을 선점하는 'Phantom Squatting' 기법을 통해 공급망 공격을 수행하고 있습니다. 또한, AI 에이전트의 명령과 콘텐츠를 구분하지 못하는 취약점을 노린 'Agentjacking'이 확산되고 있으며, Citrix NetScaler와 같은 네트워크 장비의 Zero-day 취약점을 통한 인증 우회 및 메모리 탈취 공격이 활발합니다. 전반적으로 AI 기술을 활용한 공격 자동화와 고도화된 피싱 키트(BEC-as-a-service)가 위협의 주류를 이루고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **ToddyCat**: 기업 이메일(Gmail)의 OAuth 토큰을 탈취하여 통신을 감시하는 'Umbrij' 툴 사용 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/toddycat-apt-umbrij-tool-and-oauth/120251/))
*   **The Gentlemen**: 신규 랜섬웨어 변종을 유포하며 공격적인 해커 모집 전략을 구사하는 RaaS 그룹 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/the-gentlemen-raas/120447/))
*   **CL-STA-1062**: 동남아시아 정부 및 중요 인프라를 타겟으로 'TinyRCT' 백도어를 사용하는 스파이 활동 ([Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/cl-sta-1062-tinyrct-backdoor/))
*   **Scattered Spider**: 런던 교통국(Transport for London) 공격 혐의로 주요 조직원들이 유죄를 인정함 ([Krebs on Security](https://krebsonsecurity.com/2026/06/scattered-spider-hackers-plead-guilty-on-day-1-of-trial/))

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **피엔티엠에스**: 국방 AI 지휘통제 통합 플랫폼 연구개발 사업 참여 ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207416))
*   **인젠트-한국딥러닝**: 생성형 AI 문서처리 및 RAG 기술 고도화 협력 ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207415))
*   **Kimsuky**: 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격 지속 ([이스트시큐리티](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5765))

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-49261**: MariaDB Server, CVSS 10.0 (Critical), OS Command Injection ([DNSC_RO](https://twitter.com/DNSC_RO/status/2072276555133301174))
*   **CVE-2026-8451**: Citrix NetScaler, High Severity, Memory Overread (CitrixBleed 유사) ([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/citrix-patches-netscaler-vulnerabilities-including-new-http-2-bomb-attack/))
*   **CVE-2026-33017**: Langflow, High Severity, Unauthenticated RCE ([DarkWebInformer](https://twitter.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2072023687155397020))
*   **CVE-2026-35281**: Oracle WebCenter Enterprise Capture, High Severity ([Teeeda_worker](https://twitter.com/Teeeda_worker/status/2072274124618240460))
*   **CVE-2026-46817**: Oracle E-Business Suite, High Severity, Active Exploitation ([proficioinc](https://twitter.com/proficioinc/status/2072274020502774142))

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE**: CVE-2026-49261 ([DNSC_RO](https://twitter.com/DNSC_RO/status/2072276555133301174)), CVE-2026-8451 ([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/citrix-patches-netscaler-vulnerabilities-including-new-http-2-bomb-attack/)), CVE-2026-33017 ([DarkWebInformer](https://twitter.com/DarkWebInformer/status/2072023687155397020))
*   **해시**: 이번 수집에서 특정 파일 해시값은 공개되지 않음.

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Citrix NetScaler 패치**: CVE-2026-8451 취약점 대응을 위해 즉시 최신 보안 패치를 적용하고, NSC_TASS 쿠키 관련 비정상적인 메모리 접근 로그를 모니터링하십시오.
*   **MariaDB 업데이트**: CVSS 10.0인 CVE-2026-49261 취약점 대응을 위해 MariaDB Server 버전을 즉시 업데이트하십시오.
*   **AI 에이전트 보안 점검**: 사내 AI 에이전트 및 자동화 도구의 접근 권한을 최소화하고, 외부 입력값에 대한 검증 로직(Sanitization)을 강화하여 Agentjacking 공격을 방어하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수**: 8/10
*   **근거**: 중요 네트워크 장비(Citrix) 및 데이터베이스(MariaDB)의 Critical 취약점이 공개되었고, AI를 악용한 공급망 공격이 실질적으로 발생하고 있어 즉각적인 패치와 보안 강화가 요구됨.
